itineraries.creator: empty.japan
itineraries.title: Kyoto Off the Map
itineraries.summary: Three days roaming the forests, hillside temples, and hidden markets that tourists miss entirely. No crowds, no queues — just the real Kyoto.
itineraries.best_for: Slow travellers who want nature, spirituality, and genuine discovery over highlight-reel ticking.

days.theme: Hidden temples deep in Arashiyama's back lanes

stops.title: Arashiyama Bamboo Grove + Tenryu-ji
stops.what: Walk the bamboo grove, explore the Tenryu-ji garden.
stops.why: Stunning year-round; beautiful early before tour groups arrive.
stops.tip: Get your Goshuincho stamped at Tenryu-ji (300–500 yen) — first of many on the trip.

stops.title: Otagi Nenbutsu-ji Temple
stops.what: Wander 1,200 moss-covered stone rakan statues.
stops.why: Each face is unique — you keep spotting new ones.
stops.transit_next: 20-min walk or 10-min taxi back toward Arashiyama

stops.title: Lunch in Arashiyama village
stops.what: Pick a riverside tofu or soba spot along Nakanoshima.
stops.why: Refuel before the afternoon; great views over the Oi River.

stops.title: Arancino Kyoto
stops.what: Multi-course Italian-Japanese fusion beside Takase River.
stops.why: Every course is as photogenic as it is delicious.

days.pace_note: Compact western Kyoto loop; easy walking between stops.

days.theme: Northern wilds — Kibune valley and Daisugi groves

stops.title: Kifune Shrine
stops.what: Try water Omikuji fortune-telling at the river shrine.
stops.why: Atmospheric mountain setting; ritual you won't find elsewhere.
stops.transit_next: Short walk into the surrounding cedar valley
stops.tip: Take the Eizan Railway to Kibune-guchi (35 min from Demachiyanagi) — no need to rent a car.

stops.title: Daisugi Cedar Groves
stops.what: See dozens of straight trunks rising from a single base.
stops.why: An ancient forestry technique that looks like nothing else.

stops.title: Kochidani Amida-ji Temple
stops.what: Explore a barely-visited mossy hillside temple in Ohara.
stops.why: Pure wabi-sabi — cedar, moss, total silence.
stops.transit_next: 10-min walk to Ohara village centre for dinner

stops.title: Ohara village dinner
stops.what: Simple set meal at a local shokudo in Ohara.
stops.why: Small village restaurants close early — eat here before heading back.

days.pace_note: One valley per half-day; bus connections keep it relaxed.

days.theme: Western hills ancient pines + flea market morning

stops.title: Kitano Tenmangu Flea Market (Tenjin-san)
stops.what: Browse ceramics, vintage kimono, and street food stalls.
stops.why: Beloved monthly market — a real slice of local Kyoto life.
stops.transit_next: 30-min taxi or bus west to Nishiyama
stops.tip: Market runs on the 25th of every month, 08:00–16:00 — plan your dates around it.

stops.title: Dragon Pine at Yoshiminedera Temple
stops.what: See a 600-year-old pine trained into a sweeping dragon canopy.
stops.why: One of Japan's three great pines — jaw-dropping natural sculpture.

stops.title: Rokujuan Cafe
stops.what: Warabi Mochi decorated with real flowers in a century-old townhouse.
stops.why: A rare, beautiful sweet worth the hunt.

stops.title: Farewell dinner at Arancino Kyoto
stops.what: Return to the Takase River canal for a final long dinner.
stops.why: Perfect close — every course a photograph, every bite a memory.

days.pace_note: Easy pace — market, one temple, one cafe, one dinner.

logistics.getting_around: Eizan Railway for the northern valley (Kibune); local buses and taxis for Ohara and Nishiyama. No car needed within the city.
logistics.where_to_stay: Stay in Nakagyo or Higashiyama — central enough for all three day-trip directions.
logistics.best_time: Year-round. Plum blossom (Feb) and cherry season (late Mar–Apr) add spectacular colour to most stops.
logistics.budget: Budget ~3,000–5,000 yen/day for entry fees and transport. Pick up a Goshuincho book (500–1,000 yen) for temple stamps at 300–500 yen each.

itineraries.creator: exploringlondon
itineraries.title: London's Hidden Side: 4 Days Off the Beaten Path
itineraries.hook: London that most visitors never find.
itineraries.summary: Four days across London's most surprising pockets — rooftop gardens above train stations, bombed-out church ruins, the oldest restaurant in England, and a glass floor above the Thames. Each day stays in one tight area so you walk, not rush.
itineraries.best_for: Curious explorers who want the real London — free finds, bold food, and genuine hidden gems.

days.theme: Hidden streets, ancient ruins, London's oldest restaurant

stops.title: Bombolone London
stops.what: Start the day with fresh Italian bomboloni doughnuts.
stops.why: Only dedicated bomboloni bakery in London — opens fresh daily.

stops.title: Cecil Court
stops.what: Browse the Victorian lane of rare and antique bookshops.
stops.why: Thought to have inspired Harry Potter's Diagon Alley — free to wander.
stops.transit_next: 5-min walk via the Strand to Embankment

stops.title: Victoria Embankment Gardens
stops.what: Stroll the Thames-side garden with seasonal floral displays.
stops.why: Free tulip garden minutes from the Strand — genuinely tranquil.

stops.title: St Dunstan in the East
stops.what: Sit inside the ivy-draped ruins of a Blitz-bombed medieval church.
stops.why: One of London's most hauntingly beautiful free spots — almost no crowds.
stops.transit_next: 10-min walk back toward Covent Garden via Tube
stops.tip: Go midweek to have the ruins almost to yourself.

stops.title: Rules Restaurant
stops.what: Dine at London's oldest restaurant, open since 1798.
stops.why: Ten monarchs' worth of history on the walls; classic British menu.

days.pace_note: All stops within 30 min of each other; afternoon has built-in breathing room.

days.theme: Glass floors, chimney-top views, rooftop dining

stops.title: Sistine Chapel Exhibition
stops.what: Walk through 34 life-sized Sistine Chapel reproductions near Tower Hill.
stops.why: Immersive and intimate — without the Vatican crowds.
stops.transit_next: 10-min walk along the river to Tower Bridge

stops.title: Tower Bridge Glass Walkway
stops.what: Walk the glass-floored high-level walkways above the Thames.
stops.why: Vertiginous views straight down to the river — genuinely thrilling.

stops.title: House of Habesha
stops.what: Share an Eritrean platter with Tower Bridge framed in the window.
stops.why: Vegan platter plus a stunning setting — a rare combination.
stops.transit_next: Tube or bus to Battersea, about 25 min

stops.title: Lift 109 at Battersea Power Station
stops.what: Ride a glass lift to the top of an iconic chimney stack.
stops.why: 360-degree London skyline from inside the famous power station.

stops.title: Joia at Art'otel Battersea
stops.what: Dinner at a 15th-floor rooftop with Portuguese Michelin-star cooking.
stops.why: Power Station views plus two-Michelin-starred chef Henrique Sá Pessoa.
stops.tip: Book well ahead — this rooftop fills up fast.

days.pace_note: Two riverside clusters; Battersea is the anchor so no backtracking.

days.theme: Royal gardens, world-class museums, a Victorian cafe palace

stops.title: Chelsea Physic Garden
stops.what: Wander London's oldest botanic garden, open since 1673.
stops.why: Four acres of medicinal plants and 100-year-old glasshouses.
stops.transit_next: 15-min bus or Tube to Kensington Gardens

stops.title: Kensington Palace and Sunken Garden
stops.what: Tour the working royal palace and Diana's beloved formal garden.
stops.why: Serene and historically rich — the Sunken Garden is free to view.

stops.title: The Gamble Room, V&A Museum
stops.what: Lunch inside the world's oldest museum restaurant.
stops.why: Free entry; opulent William Morris tilework you simply cannot beat.

stops.title: The Last Princesses of Punjab — Kensington Palace
stops.what: See rare artefacts from Queen Victoria's Indian goddaughter suffragette.
stops.why: Powerful exhibition open until November 2026; use code EXPLORING20 to save.
stops.tip: Book tickets in advance; use code EXPLORING20 for 20% off.

stops.title: Natural History Museum Silent Disco
stops.what: Dance among dinosaur skeletons with wireless headphones after dark.
stops.why: Three DJs, one iconic venue — a bucket-list London after-dark experience.

days.pace_note: Chelsea garden is an early start; everything else is within 15 min on foot.

days.theme: Hidden cafes, secret shops, a Piccadilly splurge

stops.title: Shot London
stops.what: Try London's most talked-about specialty coffee in Mayfair.
stops.why: Signature ice-cube coffees melt into the perfect cup as you sit.

stops.title: Alaïa London Café & Bookshop
stops.what: Find the secret third-floor café inside the Alaïa fashion house.
stops.why: Violet Cakes pastries, rare fashion books, and almost no one knows it exists.

stops.title: Waterstones Piccadilly
stops.what: Explore Europe's largest bookshop across five floors.
stops.why: 200,000+ books, a kids section, games store, and a rooftop restaurant.

stops.title: Biscuitorium at Fortnum & Mason
stops.what: Browse the new biscuit palace inside a Piccadilly institution.
stops.why: A life-sized Fortnum's replica made entirely from biscuits — 500+ hours of work.

stops.title: Nan Hot Pot
stops.what: End the trip with retro hot pot open until 3am in Leicester Square.
stops.why: Double broths, DIY dipping sauces, shaved ice desserts — pure fun.
stops.tip: Book the First Table app for 50% off food at multiple nearby restaurants if hot pot isn't your thing.

days.pace_note: All within a tight Mayfair-Piccadilly loop; lunch is relaxed browsing, not a sit-down.

logistics.getting_around: Oyster card or contactless covers all Tube, bus, and Elizabeth line travel. Most day areas are walkable once you arrive. Avoid taxis between areas.
logistics.where_to_stay: South Kensington or Covent Garden puts you central for all four days with easy Tube access in every direction.
logistics.best_time: Late April to early July for long daylight hours; many seasonal exhibitions run spring through summer 2026.
logistics.budget: Use the First Table app (code EXPLORING for £3 credit) for 50% off food at dozens of restaurants off-peak. Many anchor sites are free — the Gamble Room, Cecil Court, St Dunstan, Embankment Gardens.

itineraries.creator: exploringlondon
itineraries.title: A Perfect 2 Days in York
itineraries.hook: Medieval streets, legendary cream teas, and views over the rooftops.
itineraries.summary: York packs a thousand years of history into a walkable city centre. Two days is just enough to do it justice without rushing.
itineraries.best_for: History lovers, foodies, day-trippers from London

days.theme: The Historic Core

stops.title: The Shambles
stops.what: Wander the crooked timber-framed medieval street.
stops.why: Iconic, free, and best before the crowds arrive.
stops.tip: Go before 10am — it fills up fast and the early light makes it magical.

stops.title: Clifford's Tower
stops.what: Climb the Norman keep for rooftop views over York.
stops.why: Best panorama of the medieval skyline in the city.
stops.transit_next: 2-min walk to the Castle Museum next door

stops.title: York Castle Museum
stops.what: Explore the recreated Victorian street inside the museum.
stops.why: Genuinely immersive — feels like stepping into 1890s York.
stops.transit_next: 10-min walk north to St Helen's Square

stops.title: Betty's Cafe Tea Rooms
stops.what: Sit down for a proper cream tea and a fat rascal.
stops.why: An institution since 1919 — bucket-list York experience.

days.pace_note: All stops are under 15 min apart on foot.

days.hook: Chocolate, momos, and one last wander
days.theme: Indulgent Treats and Hidden Flavours

stops.title: Monk Bar Chocolatiers
stops.what: Try the signature hot chocolate shot in an edible cup.
stops.why: Unique to York — worth every penny.
stops.tip: The hot choc shot sells out — go early in the day.

stops.title: Tass
stops.what: Dinner of Nepalese momos before heading home.
stops.why: Generous, authentic, and under £11 — brilliant value.

days.pace_note: A lighter day — leaves time to revisit favourites or browse shops.

logistics.getting_around: York's centre is fully walkable — no car or bus needed between any of these stops.
logistics.where_to_stay: Stay central, within the city walls, to walk everywhere.
logistics.best_time: Weekday mornings are quietest; avoid bank holidays when The Shambles gets very busy.
logistics.budget: Betty's and Clifford's Tower are the main spends; the rest is low-cost or free.

itineraries.creator: raimeetravel
itineraries.title: Kyoto Nobody Tells You About
itineraries.hook: Two days hitting Kyoto's real hidden layer.
itineraries.summary: Skip the tour-bus circuit and spend two days in Kyoto's quieter corners. Sake breweries, hand-dipped candles, rooftop temples, and a kabuki night — all without the crowds.
itineraries.best_for: Curious travelers who want depth over selfie stops

days.hook: Fushimi, brewed and made by hand
days.theme: Craft and sake in Kyoto's historic brewing quarter

stops.title: Traditional Japanese Candle Workshop, Fushimi
stops.what: Paint your own hand-dipped candle at a 125-year studio.
stops.why: Hands-on craft that fills maybe 90 minutes — done before crowds arrive.
stops.tip: Book ahead — sessions fill fast and the English page can be hard to find.

stops.title: Fushimi Sake District
stops.what: Walk the brewery quarter and order the flight of 18 local sakes.
stops.why: Pure spring water fed these breweries for centuries — tastes noticeably different.
stops.transit_next: 25-min train back to central Kyoto (Kintetsu line)

stops.title: Minamizawa Theater
stops.what: Watch a full kabuki performance at Kyoto's oldest stage.
stops.why: Drama, dance, and elaborate costumes — a complete cultural evening in one stop.

days.pace_note: Three stops, one neighborhood. Afternoon is free.

days.theme: Quiet temples, hidden nature, zero crowds

stops.title: Kyoto Meditation Center
stops.what: Morning meditation, guided walk, vegetarian lunch, and matcha.
stops.why: Creator's single favorite Kyoto experience — peaceful and completely off the tourist map.
stops.transit_next: 20-min taxi or bus to Yoshiminadera area

stops.title: Yoshiminadera Temple
stops.what: Explore a centuries-old hillside temple with sweeping city views.
stops.why: Virtually no other visitors — the hidden Kyoto crowds never reach.
stops.transit_next: 30-min bus or taxi to Raksai area

stops.title: Raksai Bamboo Park
stops.what: Wander the quiet bamboo groves and peek into the production museum.
stops.why: Everything Arashiyama bamboo is, minus the selfie sticks.

days.pace_note: Morning is slow and intentional; two easy outdoor stops fill the afternoon.

logistics.getting_around: Train and bus cover most of Kyoto; taxi for the hillside temple and bamboo park.
logistics.where_to_stay: Central Kyoto keeps both days reachable without long commutes.

itineraries.creator: raimeetravel
itineraries.title: Two Days in Madrid the Right Way
itineraries.hook: Art, tapas, and parks — no rushing.
itineraries.summary: Two unhurried days anchored by world-class art and the city's best neighbourhood eating. Each day stays in one tight area so you can actually absorb it.
itineraries.best_for: First-timers who want depth over coverage

days.theme: Art and green space

stops.title: Chocolatería San Ginés
stops.what: Churros and thick hot chocolate for breakfast.
stops.why: Open since 1894 — the definitive Madrid start.
stops.transit_next: 15-min walk or short metro to Prado
stops.tip: Go early to beat the queue; it fills up fast even on weekdays.

stops.title: Museo del Prado
stops.what: Spend two hours with Velázquez, Goya, and El Greco.
stops.why: One of Europe's top museums — give it real time.

stops.title: Retiro Park
stops.what: Stroll the shaded paths and sit by the ornamental lake.
stops.why: Free, beautiful midday reset after museum intensity.
stops.transit_next: 25-min walk or metro to La Latina

stops.title: La Latina tapas hop
stops.what: Bar-hop the medieval streets for pintxos and wine.
stops.why: Best atmosphere in Madrid at golden hour — narrow streets, old bars.

days.pace_note: Four stops, two on foot. Easy full day with a built-in park break.

days.theme: Modern art and a Madrid institution lunch

stops.title: Museo Reina Sofia
stops.what: See Guernica and the Dalí and Miró collections.
stops.why: Picasso's Guernica alone is worth the visit.
stops.transit_next: 10-min walk to La Latina / Cava Baja

stops.title: Casa Lucio
stops.what: Huevos rotos — fried eggs broken over crispy potatoes.
stops.why: A Madrid institution; this dish is the reason locals have eaten here for decades.
stops.tip: Book a table ahead — this place fills up every day for lunch.

days.pace_note: Just two stops — one big museum, one legendary lunch. Leave the afternoon free.

logistics.getting_around: Metro is fast and cheap for cross-city moves; most Paseo del Arte and La Latina sights are walkable from each other.
logistics.where_to_stay: Stay near Sol, La Latina, or Paseo del Arte to keep both days walkable.
logistics.best_time: Spring (April–May) or autumn (September–October) for comfortable weather and smaller crowds.
logistics.budget: Book flights early and bundle with hotel for best rates. Check entry requirements (EES digital border checks rolling out 2026) before traveling.

itineraries.creator: thefloridaqueenie
itineraries.title: Boca Grande: Old Florida at Its Most Untouched
itineraries.hook: An island that time forgot — in the best way.
itineraries.summary: Gasparilla Island is a rare pocket of Old Florida where dolphins swim to shore and a 1913 inn still sets the tone. Two slow days, zero hustle: lighthouse views, legendary lobster rolls, and ice cream by the water.
itineraries.best_for: Couples, slow travelers, Old Florida lovers

days.hook: Lighthouse, library, and Old Florida charm
days.theme: Historic Island Landmarks

stops.title: Gasparilla Island Lighthouse
stops.what: Climb 132 steps for sweeping Gulf sunrise views.
stops.why: Built 1881, relocated from Delaware — genuinely one-of-a-kind.
stops.tip: Go early before the heat builds — the top gets warm fast.

stops.title: Johann Fust Community Library
stops.what: Browse rare DuPont shells and the peaceful outdoor sitting area.
stops.why: A 1949 island library unlike any library you've ever been inside.

stops.title: Newlin's Gourmet
stops.what: Order the lobster roll — full stop.
stops.why: Creator says it beats Maine; regulars agree.

stops.title: Gasparilla Inn and Club
stops.what: Stroll the grand veranda and manicured grounds.
stops.why: A free glimpse into 1913 Old Florida luxury.

stops.title: The Pink Pony
stops.what: Pick up an ice cream cone, walk the village.
stops.why: Boca Grande's beloved institution for generations.

days.pace_note: All stops within easy walking distance; very relaxed pace.

days.hook: Wildlife, water, and dinner worth staying for
days.theme: Nature, Marina, and a Pink Night Out

stops.title: Boca Grande Beach
stops.what: Swim or wade — watch for manatees at your ankles.
stops.why: Crystal Gulf water with near-guaranteed wildlife sightings.
stops.tip: Get here before 10 am — best wildlife activity is early morning.

stops.title: Boca Grande Rail Trail
stops.what: Walk or kayak under the historic tracks over Gulf waters.
stops.why: Dilapidated train tracks over the water — dolphins surface here.

stops.title: Whidden's Marina
stops.what: Wander Florida's oldest marina, maritime museum, and gift shop.
stops.why: Family-run since 1925 — pure, unfiltered Old Florida.

stops.title: The Pink Elephant Restaurant
stops.what: Dinner with pink decor, great food, and a keepsake elephant charm.
stops.why: Every cocktail comes with a tiny elephant charm to take home.

days.pace_note: Morning is active; afternoon is slow — save energy for dinner.

logistics.getting_around: Boca Grande is tiny — walk, bike, or golf cart everything. No rideshare needed.
logistics.where_to_stay: The Gasparilla Inn and Club is the splurge; smaller inns and vacation rentals dot the island for budget options.
logistics.best_time: Winter through early spring (Nov–April) for mild weather and peak tarpon season.
logistics.budget: Mid-range to splurge; the beach and rail trail are free, Newlin's and The Pink Elephant are the main spends.

itineraries.creator: thefloridaqueenie
itineraries.title: Sarasota in 2 Days: Circus Palaces & Quartz Sand
itineraries.hook: Gulf Coast magic — from a Gilded Age estate to the whitest sand in the US.
itineraries.summary: Two very different Sarasota days: one anchored downtown at the Ringling estate and botanical gardens, the other entirely on Siesta Key with a kayak through a wild tidal pass and a sunset dinner on the patio. Neither day asks much of you — just enough to feel like a real Florida insider.

days.theme: Art, wonder, and bayfront greenery

stops.title: Toastique
stops.what: Grab an avocado toast and cold-pressed juice.
stops.why: Quickest, freshest fuel before a big morning.
stops.transit_next: 10-min drive north to the Ringling estate.
stops.tip: Go Monday — The Ringling is free all day.

stops.title: The Ringling Museum
stops.what: Tour the circus museum, Baroque galleries, and bayfront palace.
stops.why: Baroque art + full circus history on one free Monday estate.

stops.title: Marie Selby Botanical Gardens
stops.what: Wander orchid collections, koi ponds, and bayfront paths.
stops.why: The only botanical garden on earth dedicated to orchid research.

stops.title: Marietta Museum of Art and Whimsy
stops.what: Explore the stained glass, ironworks, and butterfly gardens.
stops.why: Free, offbeat, and genuinely makes you forget your problems.
stops.tip: Open Thu–Sat, 1–4 pm only (Nov–May). Confirm hours before visiting.

days.pace_note: Two anchors close together; afternoon is leisurely and free.

days.hook: Wild pass, white sand, sunset patio
days.theme: Siesta Key end to end

stops.title: Focaccia Sandwich and Bakery
stops.what: Pick up a fresh-baked focaccia sandwich for the beach.
stops.why: Siesta Key's most inventive lunch — grab early before it sells out.
stops.transit_next: 15-min drive south to Turtle Beach / Midnight Pass.

stops.title: Midnight Pass
stops.what: Kayak from Turtle Beach through mangroves into the reopened tidal pass.
stops.why: A once-closed wild pass that recently reopened — rare Florida experience.
stops.transit_next: 10-min drive north up Siesta Key to the main beach.
stops.tip: Kayak rentals available on-site at Turtle Beach Park.

stops.title: Siesta Key Beach
stops.what: Swim and relax on quartz sand that stays cool underfoot.
stops.why: Voted top US beach — 99% quartz, crystal-clear turquoise water.

stops.title: The Cottage on Siesta Key
stops.what: Dinner with live music on the back patio.
stops.why: Best sunset-to-evening spot on the Key — strong drinks, great vibe.

days.pace_note: One island all day; paddle in the morning, beach by noon, done.

logistics.getting_around: A car is essential — Siesta Key and the Ringling estate are not walkable from each other. Rideshare works for downtown Sarasota stops.
logistics.where_to_stay: Downtown Sarasota puts you close to Day 1 stops; Siesta Key Village is ideal if you want to walk to the beach on Day 2.
logistics.best_time: November through April for dry weather and comfortable temperatures; avoid peak summer heat if possible.
logistics.budget: Day 1 can be nearly free (Ringling on Monday, Marietta Museum free). Day 2 costs are mainly kayak rental (~$30–40) and meals.

itineraries.creator: thefloridaqueenie
itineraries.title: Tampa Like a Local: Hyde Park, Downtown & the Best Bites
itineraries.hook: Two days, zero filler — just Tampa's best food, views, and waterfront vibes.
itineraries.summary: This itinerary anchors Day 1 in Hyde Park with bikes, tacos, and a steakhouse dinner. Day 2 swings downtown for the aquarium, Oxford Exchange, and rooftop cocktails at golden hour.
itineraries.best_for: Food lovers, couples, first-time Tampa visitors

days.theme: Neighborhood eats and waterfront golden hour

stops.title: Elevage Restaurant
stops.what: Sit-down breakfast at the Epicurean Hotel.
stops.why: Those potatoes have a fan base for good reason.
stops.tip: Stay at the Epicurean Hotel to roll out of bed directly into this breakfast.

stops.title: Bar Taco
stops.what: Casual lunch tacos at Hyde Park Village.
stops.why: She went back two days straight — enough said.

stops.title: Bayshore Boulevard
stops.what: Rent bikes from the Epicurean and ride the waterfront path.
stops.why: World's longest sidewalk, waterfront mansions, golden-hour light.

stops.title: Meat Market Tampa
stops.what: Dinner: filet mignon and cookies-and-milk dessert.
stops.why: Both rated 10/10 — the best dinner anchor in Hyde Park.

days.pace_note: Four stops, one neighborhood — no car needed all day.

days.hook: Downtown, fish tanks, and rooftop views
days.theme: Culture, coffee, and cocktails at sunset

stops.title: Oxford Exchange
stops.what: Morning coffee inside Tampa's most beautiful bookstore.
stops.why: Champagne bar, tea bar, and stunning interiors — a hidden gem.
stops.transit_next: 15-min drive or TECO streetcar to Channelside
stops.tip: The TECO Line Streetcar is free and connects downtown hotels to Channelside — skip parking.

stops.title: Florida Aquarium
stops.what: Explore mesmerizing marine tanks for a couple of hours.
stops.why: Genuinely captivating for adults, not just families.
stops.transit_next: 5-min walk along the waterfront to Sparkman Wharf

stops.title: Sparkman Wharf
stops.what: Lunch at outdoor waterfront food stalls; grab a craft beer.
stops.why: Buzzy beer garden on the water — perfect midday reset.
stops.transit_next: 5-min walk to JW Marriott Water Street

stops.title: Beacon Tampa
stops.what: Sunset cocktails on the 27th-floor rooftop.
stops.why: Panoramic Tampa Bay views — the perfect send-off.

days.pace_note: All stops within a 10-min walk of each other downtown.

logistics.getting_around: Use the free TECO Line Streetcar for downtown; rent bikes from the Epicurean Hotel for Bayshore Boulevard. A car is handy for Hyde Park to downtown transfers.
logistics.where_to_stay: Epicurean Hotel in Hyde Park — culinary-themed boutique with rooftop pool, rain showers, and premium beds right on S Howard Ave.

itineraries.creator: thefloridaqueenie
itineraries.title: Venice, FL: Shark Teeth & Small-Town Gulf Magic
itineraries.hook: A slow Gulf town with prehistoric beaches and zero theme-park crowds.
itineraries.summary: Venice packs a surprising amount of charm into two easy days. Fossil-dark sand, a free circus museum, and a sunset drum circle — all walkable, all genuinely local.

days.theme: Historic Downtown + Venice Beach

stops.title: Venice Train Depot & Ringling Brothers Circus Museum
stops.what: Free guided tour of the original circus train car.
stops.why: The only Ringling wintering depot left — and it's free.
stops.tip: Tours are informal and short — arrive right at open to catch a guide before the midday heat.

stops.title: The Soda Fountain
stops.what: Grab a head-sized pizza slice at this 1950s downtown joint.
stops.why: Best-value lunch in downtown — kitschy, fast, filling.
stops.transit_next: 10-min walk west down Venice Ave to the beach

stops.title: Venice Avenue Stroll
stops.what: Walk the banyan-canopied boulevard to Venice Beach.
stops.why: Prettiest approach to the Gulf — shade-covered and photo-worthy.

stops.title: Venice Beach
stops.what: Swim, comb the shoreline for shark teeth, catch the Gulf sun.
stops.why: Prehistoric teeth wash up here daily — bring a mesh bag.

stops.title: Venice Beach Drum Circle
stops.what: Join the free sunset drum circle at the beach pavilion.
stops.why: A nightly local tradition — the most joyful way to end a Gulf day.

days.pace_note: All stops within 10-min walk; long afternoon beach break built in.

days.hook: Fossil beach, craft beer, pier drinks
days.theme: Caspersen Beach + Local Eats

stops.title: Caspersen Beach
stops.what: Hunt shark teeth in the dark fossil-sand shallows.
stops.why: Venice's best tooth-hunting spot — handfuls guaranteed in rocky shallows.
stops.transit_next: 10-min drive or rideshare north to pier district
stops.tip: Go early before the tide comes in — low tide exposes the most fossil fragments.

stops.title: Sharky's Beach
stops.what: Casual Gulf-view lunch at the iconic beachfront spot.
stops.why: Breezy pier views and a relaxed local vibe — the right midday energy.
stops.transit_next: 5-min walk or short drive to Three Bridges Brewing

stops.title: Three Bridges Brewing
stops.what: Afternoon pint, cider, or smash burger in the dog-friendly outdoor taproom.
stops.why: Venice's best local brewery — cornhole, craft beer, and good vibes.
stops.transit_next: 5-min drive back to pier for sunset

stops.title: Finn's on the Pier
stops.what: Wind down with a craft cocktail next to the Venice Fishing Pier.
stops.why: Gulf breeze and creative cocktails — the upscale note to end the trip.

days.pace_note: Morning beach is the anchor; afternoon is low-key eating and drinking.

logistics.getting_around: Venice is very walkable downtown; a car or rideshare is handy for Caspersen Beach (~2 mi south).
logistics.where_to_stay: Inn at the Beach — steps from Venice Beach and a short walk to all of downtown.
logistics.best_time: Shoulder season (Oct–Apr) for cooler temps and calmer Gulf; avoid summer heat and afternoon storms.
logistics.budget: Most highlights are free (beaches, drum circle, circus museum); budget $30–60/day for food and drinks.
